items.headline: City addresses affordability protections for subsidized housing projects
items.summary: The Council considered new policies on affordability restrictions for subsidized housing if project-based subsidy contracts expire or properties face foreclosure. This directly affects whether affordable housing units remain affordable for low-income San José residents.

items.headline: City Council reviews $2026-27 capital improvement projects
items.summary: The council held a study session to examine the Capital Improvement Program for fiscal year 2026-27, which outlines major city infrastructure and development projects. This preview allows residents to understand what public works and improvements are planned for the upcoming year.

items.headline: City Council to Consider Changes to Tenant Protection Rules
items.summary: Sunnyvale is introducing an ordinance that would amend its Residential Tenant Protections Program. This could affect renter rights and landlord obligations in the city.

items.headline: City releases children and youth services master plan status
items.summary: The City Council received an update on its Children and Youth Services Master Plan, which guides programs like the Safe Summer Initiative and Youth Intervention Services. This plan shapes what youth programs and services San José residents can access.

items.headline: Library facilities and customer experience plan moves forward
items.summary: The City Council reviewed the status of the Library Facilities and Customer Experience Plan on April 9. This plan will determine future improvements to San José's library system and how library services are delivered to residents.

items.headline: Downtown historic building could get major floor space bonus
items.summary: The building at 405 Kipling Street in downtown Palo Alto is being considered for elevation from Category 4 to Category 2 historic status, which would allow 5,000 additional square feet of floor area as incentive for seismic and historic rehabilitation work.

items.headline: Major mixed-use development proposed for San Antonio Road
items.summary: A rezoning proposal would allow an 8-story building with 167 apartments (including 28 affordable units) and 1,400 sq ft of commercial space on San Antonio Road. The City Council will review whether to rezone the currently commercial properties for this mixed-use development.

items.headline: Mountain View zoning hearing postponed; 333 Franklin Street project delayed
items.summary: The Administrative Zoning hearing scheduled for April 8 was cancelled and rescheduled to April 22. A project at 333 Franklin Street that was on the April 8 agenda will be re-noticed with a new hearing date, meaning any decision on that development is delayed.
